interactions.teaches: Allows manipulation of the viewing angle to understand the 3D structure and gradients of the Gaussian surface

interactions.label: Plane height
interactions.teaches: Adjusting the plane height demonstrates how different slices of the Gaussian surface relate to its contours and values

demo_steps.title: Initial Gaussian View
demo_steps.narration: Here is the 3D Gaussian surface with its height represented through a color gradient. Notice how the surface smoothly peaks at the center and falls off towards the edges.

demo_steps.title: Exploring Heights
demo_steps.narration: By changing the plane height, you can see the cross-section of the Gaussian at different values. At each height, the contour lines on the plane change, reflecting the underlying surface.

demo_steps.title: Understanding Gradient
demo_steps.narration: Rotate the view to see the slope and curvature of the surface. The color gradient helps visualize the steepness and location of the peak relative to the base.

plan.camera_suggestion: A slightly elevated perspective from one corner to capture the full Gaussian surface and the reference plane, oriented to match the original figure's silhouette.

plan.notes: Ensure color gradients accurately reflect height variations. Gaussian should be rendered as a high-density mesh for smoothness. Labels must be positioned to avoid overlap with critical geometry features.

elements: 2D Gaussian surface with color gradient
x-axis derivative of the Gaussian
xy-plane

interactions.label: Sigma
interactions.teaches: Adjusting sigma changes the width of the Gaussian, demonstrating how derivatives at different scales emphasize various image aspects.

demo_steps.title: Standard Gaussian, sigma=4
demo_steps.narration: This is a 2D Gaussian surface with sigma=4. The surface represents how a Gaussian distribution spreads over space.

demo_steps.title: Narrow Gaussian, sigma=2
demo_steps.narration: Reducing sigma to 2 narrows the Gaussian, highlighting fine details. This shows how smaller scales focus on finer features.

demo_steps.title: Wide Gaussian, sigma=8
demo_steps.narration: Increasing sigma to 8 widens the Gaussian, emphasizing broader object boundaries. Larger scales highlight more general features.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Isometric view capturing the entire Gaussian surface and derivative with a moderate zoom level to ensure all features are visible

elements: 2D Gaussian surface with correct color gradient
x-axis derivative of the Gaussian
high-order derivative annotations
xy-plane with mirror boundary extension

interactions.label: Sigma
interactions.teaches: Adjusting sigma modifies the Gaussian width, illustrating how different scales affect image feature emphasis.

demo_steps.title: Initial Gaussian, sigma=4
demo_steps.narration: Currently displayed is the Gaussian surface with sigma=4. It has a moderate spread, emphasizing certain image features while maintaining detail balance.

demo_steps.title: Refined details, sigma=2
demo_steps.narration: Shrinking sigma to 2 compresses the Gaussian, making it steeper and highlighting finer structures. This effect is crucial for capturing intricate image details.

demo_steps.title: Broad boundaries, sigma=8
demo_steps.narration: Expanding sigma to 8 results in a broader Gaussian, which smoothens the surface and accentuates larger image structures like boundaries, filtering out noise.

plan.camera_suggestion: Overhead isometric view focusing on the Gaussian surface, accurately folded to match the original's aspect with careful zoom to maintain clarity of all features
